sourceTitle: Embeds in Notion: A comprehensive guide for teams

questions.question: Which of the following content types can be embedded in Notion by simply pasting its link?

options.text: Google Drive files
options.explanation: Google Drive files can be embedded by pasting the file link, as described in the guide.

options.text: PDF files from your computer
options.explanation: PDFs must be uploaded from your hard drive; they cannot be embedded by link alone.

options.text: Local images stored on your device
options.explanation: Local images need to be uploaded; pasting a link only works for web‑hosted resources.

options.text: Notion page URLs
options.explanation: While you can link to other Notion pages, that creates a hyperlink, not an embed.

questions.question: What is a primary benefit of using Notion’s embedded services for a team?

options.text: It eliminates the need for any external apps entirely
options.explanation: Embedding consolidates tools but does not replace them; external apps are still needed for full functionality.

options.text: It reduces the need to switch between multiple apps and browser tabs
options.explanation: The guide states that embedding helps keep information in one place, minimizing context switching.

options.text: It automatically backs up all embedded content to Notion’s servers
options.explanation: Backup is not mentioned as a benefit of embedding.

options.text: It increases the loading speed of Notion pages
options.explanation: Embedding can actually add load time depending on the external content; speed increase is not a claimed benefit.

questions.question: How can you embed a PDF document into a Notion page?

options.text: Paste the PDF’s URL from a cloud storage service
options.explanation: The guide specifies uploading the file from your hard drive, not pasting a link.

options.text: Upload the PDF file directly from your computer or attach it as a file block
options.explanation: Both uploading and attaching as a file block are the methods described for PDFs.

options.text: Convert the PDF to an image and embed the image
options.explanation: Converting to an image is unnecessary; Notion supports PDFs natively.

options.text: Embed the PDF using an iframe code snippet
options.explanation: Iframe embedding is not mentioned; Notion uses its own file block system for PDFs.

questions.question: When a Typeform form that’s embedded in Notion is edited in Typeform, what happens to the embedded version?

options.text: The embedded form remains unchanged until you re‑embed it
options.explanation: The guide notes that changes update automatically.

options.text: The embedded form automatically reflects the updates made in Typeform
options.explanation: Embedded Typeform forms sync with the live version, so edits appear instantly.

options.text: Notion shows a warning that the form is out‑of‑date
options.explanation: No warning mechanism is described in the article.

options.text: The form stops working until you refresh the Notion page
options.explanation: Refresh is not required; updates propagate automatically.

questions.question: Which embedded video type lets you adjust playback speed directly within Notion?

options.text: YouTube videos
options.explanation: The guide states that embedded YouTube videos can be played and their playback speed adjusted.

options.text: Loom videos
options.explanation: While Loom videos can be embedded and played, the guide does not mention playback‑speed control for Loom.

options.text: Google Maps embeds
options.explanation: Google Maps is not a video; speed control is irrelevant.

options.text: PDF embeds
options.explanation: PDFs are static documents, not videos.

questions.question: After embedding a Google Maps address, which actions can you perform on the map within Notion?

options.text: Resize the map block and zoom in or out
options.explanation: The article explicitly mentions that the map can be resized and zoomed.

options.text: Change the map’s visual style (satellite, terrain, etc.)
options.explanation: Changing map style is not listed as a supported feature.

options.text: Add custom markers directly from Notion
options.explanation: Custom marker addition is not described as an embed capability.

options.text: Edit the address text directly on the map
options.explanation: The embed shows a static map based on the pasted link; address editing must be done in Google Maps.

questions.question: A team wants to embed a Google Drive spreadsheet so that members can edit the sheet directly within Notion. Based on the guide, what limitation should they expect?

options.text: Notion only displays a preview of the spreadsheet; editing must be done in Google Drive
options.explanation: The guide describes embedding by pasting the link, which creates a preview. Full editing capabilities remain in the original Google Drive app.

options.text: The spreadsheet will lose all formulas when embedded
options.explanation: The guide does not mention any loss of data or formulas upon embedding.

options.text: Embedding a spreadsheet requires a paid Notion plan
options.explanation: No pricing restriction is mentioned for embedding Google Drive files.

options.text: The embedded spreadsheet will automatically sync changes made in Notion back to Google Drive
options.explanation: Syncing edits from Notion to Google Drive is not supported; the embed is read‑only.
